data.title: Glenview adds new Pinterest, Zillow stakes, exits Wayfair, ON in Q3 moves
data.description:
Gleview Capital took now stakes in Pinterest (PINS), with 808K shares, Zillow (Z), with 890K shares, and Black (XYZ), with 322K shares, during Q3 2025, the hedge fund disclosed on Friday.

The firm exited its positions in Wayfair (W), as well as ON Semiconductor (ON) and Sandisk (SNDK).

It boosted its holdings in Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (TEVA), to 16.69M shares in Q3 from 15.82M in Q2, and Amazon (AMZN), to 652K from 563K in Q2.

Trimmed positions include Meta Platforms (META), to 12K shares in Q3 from 116K in the prior quarter, and Alight (ALIT), to 20.38M from 24.38M in Q2, the 13F filing showed.

data.title: Vizsla Copper upsizes non-brokered private placement to $42M
data.description:
• Vizsla Copper (VCU:CA) announced on Friday that it has increased the size of its non-brokered private placement from up to $25M to approximately $42M. 
  • The offering will include 23.14M  and 6.48M post-consolidation common shares of the company at a price of $1.08 per LIFE share and $1.08 per common share for gross proceeds of up to $25M and $7M, respectively.
  • Furthermore, flow-through post-consolidation common shares at a price of $1.24 per FT share and charity flow-through post-consolidation shares at a price of $1.72 per CFT share for gross proceeds of approximately $10M.
  • The company plans to use the gross proceeds to incur Canadian critical minerals exploration expenses.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the common shares and the LIFE shares will be used for exploration of the Palmer Project, continued exploration on the company's mineral properties in British Columbia, with a principal focus on the Poplar copper-gold project, costs of acquisition of the Palmer Project, and general working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 4, 2025.

data.title: Thinkific announces renewal of normal course issuer bid for common shares
data.description:
• Thinkific Labs (THNC:CA) said on Friday that the Toronto Stock Exchange has accepted its notice to renew our Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB).
  • Under the NCIB, the company can purchase for cancellation up to an aggregate of 3.40M common shares in the capital of Thinkific, representing about 5% of the issued and outstanding shares as of November 5, 2025, being the last trading day prior to the submission of the initial Notice to the TSX. As of November 5, 2025, Thinkific had a total of 67.9M shares issued and outstanding.
  • The NCIB will commence on November 19, 2025, and terminate on November 18, 2026, or earlier if the maximum number of Shares under the NCIB have been purchased or if the NCIB has been terminated.
  • Under the NCIB, other than purchases made under a block purchase exception in accordance with the rules and policies of the TSX, Thinkific may acquire, from time to time, up to 12,234 shares per day on the TSX, being 25% of the average daily trading volume of the Shares for the period from May 1, 2025 to October 31, 2025, being 48,936 Shares.
  • In connection with the NCIB, the company intends to enter into an automatic share purchase plan on or about November 19, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Taitron to voluntary delist from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Taitron Components (TAIT) on Friday reported Q3 EPS of -$0.01 and Net product revenue of $529,000.
  • The company declared cash dividend of $0.035 per common share.
  • Taitron also announced the resignation of its CFO David Vanderhorst, effective immediately.
  • Additionally, the company said that it will voluntarily delist its common stock from Nasdaq and suspend its public reporting obligations.
  • Taitron said that its costs of maintaining the Nasdaq listing and remaining a public reporting company, including costs of compliance, the demands on management time and the company resources required to maintain its listed and registered status, outweigh the benefits to the company and its stockholders of continued Nasdaq listing and SEC reporting.
  • It will file a Form 25 with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or about November 24, 2025, and the Nasdaq delisting is expected to become effective on or about December 4, 2025, at which time trading on Nasdaq will cease.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fed's not moving in direction of goals on both mandates, Bostic says
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic said Friday that on both of the central bank's mandates -- full employment and stable prices -- "we are not moving in a direction that's getting us to target."

He reiterated that the Fed faces difficult trade-offs between supporting employment and controlling inflation.

And the recent lack of official data because of the government shutdown makes it "an incredibly challenging environment to be doing policy," Bostic said in a fireside chat at an industry conference.

Bostic, who recently announced plans to retire at the end of his current term in February 2026, noted that he was able to support the Fed's last two rate cuts. He said he hopes the December meeting will be accompanied by "a lot of data" to properly assess the policy path.

data.title: Capital Bancorp appoints Jacob Dalaya as CFO
data.description:
• Capital Bancorp (NASDAQ:CBNK)  announced on Friday the appointment of Jacob Dalaya as Executive Vice President and CFO, effective immediately.
  • Dalaya brings in his vast expertise in strategic and financial planning and in-depth knowledge of the industry.
  • The company has $3.4 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025.
